title: Apple sues OpenAI for alleged theft of hardware trade secrets
summary: The Cupertino company accuses OpenAI of hiring former employees who stole confidential information about chips and hardware.

title: EU threatens Meta with multi-million fines for addictive features on Instagram and Facebook
summary: Brussels demands Meta disable autoplay and infinite scroll or face sanctions of up to $12 billion.

title: OpenAI's head of security leaves the company
summary: The departure of the executive in charge of security comes amid regulatory and trust challenges.

title: Meta removes AI feature that generated images from public Instagram photos
summary: The decision comes after criticism over the unauthorized use of user images to train models.

title: New York bans deceptive subscription practices and hidden fees
summary: The city becomes the first in the US to prevent companies from making it difficult to cancel recurring subscriptions.

title: OpenAI introduces GPT-5.6 Sol, an AI model capable of self-training others and proving theorems
summary: The system, with five levels of reasoning, can post-train smaller models and has solved the double cycle cover conjecture.

title: Tencent negotiates purchase of Manus from Meta after Chinese government intervention
summary: The deal would return independence to Manus after Beijing forced Meta to unwind its $2 billion investment.

title: SK Hynix makes largest foreign IPO in US history with $26.5 billion
summary: The South Korean memory manufacturer plans to use the funds to expand HBM production and build new factories in the country.

title: EU approves 'Chat Control' to scan private messages without a warrant
summary: The regulation allows companies like Meta and Google to analyze communications for illegal content, drawing criticism for mass surveillance.

title: AMD confirms launch of Zen 6 processors within the next two weeks
summary: The first EPYC 'Venice' chips with the new architecture will arrive earlier than expected, according to the company.

title: Intel Core Ultra 5 245K hits all-time low price of $179
summary: The 14-core processor with PCIe 5.0 support is offered at a 42% discount from its original price.

title: They manage to run Minecraft in 3D with textures on an original Game Boy
summary: A developer has gotten the game to run on nearly 30-year-old hardware, leveraging its limitations to create a three-dimensional effect.

title: Ransom negotiator who helped the BlackCat gang sentenced to 70 months in prison
summary: The defendant, who also pleaded guilty in a case in Florida, facilitated extortions against US companies.

title: Grok 4.5 and Muse Spark 1.1 offer high performance at low cost compared to competition
summary: The new AI models from xAI and Muse promise 90% effectiveness at just 20% of the price of their rivals, plus a 1 million token context.

title: OpenAI shuts down its Atlas browser after eight months and merges it with ChatGPT
summary: The company simplifies its product offering by integrating Atlas' capabilities into its main chatbot, eliminating the brand.

title: OpenAI could run out of funds by mid-2027 according to financial analysis
summary: The report indicates that operating expenses exceed revenue and the company will need a new funding round to sustain itself.

title: SpaceX plans to launch 100,000 Starlink satellites to multiply bandwidth by 100
summary: Elon Musk's company seeks to massively expand its constellation to offer global high-speed internet.

title: China accelerates in reusable rockets and closes the gap with SpaceX achievements
summary: Chinese advances in reusable launch vehicle technology narrow the gap with Elon Musk's Falcon rockets.

title: Anthropic manages to read the 'thoughts' of its AI Claude in a groundbreaking study
summary: Researchers observed a global workspace in the model, revealing for the first time how it internally processes information.

title: Disney+ considers launching a free ad-supported tier to compete in the streaming market
summary: The platform could follow the freemium model adopted by other companies to attract more users.
